compiled_state.author: Joe Oakhart

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
PRE-FLIP GATE: FAIL (2026-07-17T01:58:48Z) — FLIP HELD, do not enable enforcement yet.
Validation window C0=7141adf3c..9484fc29a found 2 unsigned non-SNAPSHOT in-scope events: a STATUS+EDIT (a CLAIM) on task 2ad8-f56e-7bc2-4948, author 594c, from agent session 5c2eef83-9ceb-4320-ad07-f54047bb7399 (harness claude-code_2-1-212_agent) at 2026-07-17T01:53:52Z — a DIFFERENT concurrent Claude agent working this shared store on an OLD pre-0ba4 build (its worktree venv predates the finalize_event fix, so txn.claim_core still writes unsigned).
This is the multi-agent reality: several agents share this dogfooding store on MIXED builds. My local (global kickstarted + venv) writers sign; CI reconciler + Gerrit review-bot already signed (own paths); but concurrent agents on old worktree venvs still emit unsigned txn events.
BLOCKER: the flip is safe ONLY once every concurrent writer runs the post-0ba4 build (527a6e730). Resolution: those agents must rebase their worktrees onto current main + reinstall (the global build auto-updates hourly, so fresh agent invocations are already fixed; the persistent risk is long-lived worktree venvs). RE-RUN the pre-flip validation window; flip only when it reports 0 unsigned non-SNAPSHOT events. The gate correctly prevented breaking a live writer — this is the safety mechanism working as designed.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body:
FLIP pushed as Gerrit change 759 (https://rebar.solutions.navateam.com/c/rebar/+/759). rebar.toml: require_authenticated=true + enforce_since=8dcef776c3dd9e68935b74b82137e1ffd8f04f10, in one change (2026-07-17T03:11:05Z).

ENFORCEMENT IS REAL (demonstrated in isolated temp stores, no shared-store pollution):
- Post-boundary UNSIGNED event -> 'rebar verify-identity --all --require-authenticated' exits 1 (2 unsigned in scope, enforced). Enforcement is real, not advisory.
- A store with ONLY pre-boundary unsigned events -> exits 0 (correctly grandfathered).
- The local WRITE-GATE refuses an unsignable non-exempt write under require_authenticated (proven by test_identity_txn_signing_heldout.py::test_unsignable_transition_is_refused_under_enforcement, landed in 0ba4).
- The landed regression test test_identity_enforce_since_grandfather.py asserts the same boundary behavior (pre grandfathered / post enforced) via the config/env path.

UNDER-ENFORCEMENT WRITER SURFACES (all sign + verify): local (79da), CI reconcile-bridge (4dc0, event 8b72ddf6), Gerrit review-bot LINK (1a51). CI's Verified gate on change 759 runs verify-identity with require_authenticated=true + the pinned anchor over the whole store — a green Verified is the authoritative confirmation that AC1 holds (all pre-boundary grandfathered, all post-boundary verified) and no writer surface goes red.

ROLLBACK (fast-revert): if a writer surface unexpectedly fails post-flip, set [identity] require_authenticated=false in rebar.toml (leave enforce_since pinned) and land it as a single Gerrit change (push gerrit HEAD:refs/for/main, Submit once Verified+1). No data cleanup — the gate returns to advisory immediately. Env override REBAR_IDENTITY_REQUIRE_AUTHENTICATED=0 disables it per-run in the interim.

comments.author: Joe Oakhart
comments.body: FLIP CI (change 759) RED — Verified -1. LLM-Review +1. CI failure is NOT verify-identity — it is one INTEGRATION test: tests/interfaces/store/test_enrich_drain_prune_concurrency.py::test_enrich_queue_prune_never_drops_concurrent_locked_writes (intended=250 persisted=144 lost=106 swallowed_write_raises=106). Root cause: that test spawns subprocess writers that write SIGNATURE/REVIEW_RESULT events to a fresh store with NO signing configured; under require_authenticated=true the write-gate correctly REFUSES the unsignable writes and the drain swallows the raises -> the concurrency invariant (prune never drops writes) sees them as lost. It is a concurrency test, orthogonal to enforcement, that relied on the OLD default (require_authenticated=false). Fix (part of this flip): the worker _prune_race_worker.py must run enforcement-agnostic (set REBAR_IDENTITY_REQUIRE_AUTHENTICATED=0, like it already sets REBAR_SYNC_PUSH=off). In production the review-bot signs (SSM key) so its writes are not refused. NOT a verify-identity/anchor problem — the anchor grandfathering is fine. Amending the flip change with the test fix + re-pushing.

compiled_state.description:
## Why

Enforcement is the epic's goal; it must land **only after** every writer signs (79da local,
4dc0 CI, 1a51 Gerrit) and grandfathering is proven (8d91). Flipping early turns every push red
by construction (bug `27ae-8322-9278-4a7c`): with 0 signed events store-wide today, the gate
would reject everything. This is the final, atomic switch — and the **only** change that writes
both the flag and `enforce_since`.

**Depends on bug `0ba4-6a28-7a88-47b2` (txn signing).** The transactional write path
(claim/transition/close, and delete) previously wrote UNSIGNED in-scope STATUS/EDIT/ARCHIVED
events (they bypassed the signing seam). 0ba4 centralizes signing in a shared `finalize_event`
choke point every write routes through. The flip is unsafe until that fix is **landed AND live
across every active writer build** (local auto-updates hourly; CI/Gerrit deploy from `main`) —
otherwise a still-old writer keeps emitting unsigned events that the merge-gate rejects. Hence
the pre-flip validation AC below: prove no active process is still writing unsigned non-SNAPSHOT
events before flipping.

## What

- In `rebar.toml`, in **one** change, set `[identity] require_authenticated = true` **and**
  `enforce_since = <the anchor SHA documented by 8d91>` (replacing the current rollout-prep pin
  `ce3f8bcbe13d37e93a0758f7f73cb3ebeac3b043`). No earlier child modifies `enforce_since`; its
  value equals the exact anchor SHA from 8d91.
- **Demonstrate enforcement is real** without polluting the shared store:
  (i) the **merge-gate** fails on a synthetic post-boundary forged/unsigned in-scope event in an
  isolated temp store (`rebar verify-identity --all --require-authenticated` exits non-zero) —
  reusing 8d91's harness; and (ii) the **local write-gate** refuses an unsignable write when the
  signing key is unset (with `require_authenticated=true`, a non-exempt write with no resolvable
  signing key is refused, exit non-zero) — proving the flag is live, not advisory.
- **Confirm each writer surface still passes under enforcement**: a representative signed write
  on local and CI verifies; the Gerrit-side in-scope writer (the review-bot LINK, per 1a51)
  verifies. No red pushes on `main`.
- **Provide a documented fast-revert (rollback) path**: how to revert
  `identity.require_authenticated` to `false` in a single change if a writer surface unexpectedly
  fails post-flip.

## Scope

The single atomic config change (flag + boundary) + under-enforcement verification + a rollback
procedure. Depends on 79da, 4dc0, 1a51, 8d91. `file_impact`: `rebar.toml` (the flag +
`enforce_since`); plus a runbook/rollback note.

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] **PRE-FLIP GATE — no active process writes an unsigned non-SNAPSHOT in-scope event.**
      Before flipping, validate over a window in which EVERY active writer operates — a fresh
      local session write, a `reconcile-bridge` CI run, a Gerrit review-bot write (its
      `relates_to` LINK on a work ticket), the **enrich drain**, and the **reconciler** — that
      no active process is still emitting unsigned in-scope events. Method: capture
      `C0 = git -C .tickets-tracker rev-parse origin/tickets`; let the writers run (or trigger
      each); fetch `origin/tickets`; then enumerate every event file introduced in
      `C0..origin/tickets` and assert **every in-scope event carries `author_sig` EXCEPT
      `*-SNAPSHOT.json`** — SNAPSHOT is the sole permitted unsigned type (a compaction artifact
      the merge-gate re-verifies via the snapshot's `authorship_ledger`, not the SNAPSHOT event).
      Proving commands (both must hold):
      (a) `rebar verify-identity --all --require-authenticated --since C0` exits 0 (every
      enforced event since `C0` verifies); and
      (b) no unsigned non-SNAPSHOT event exists in the window — e.g.
      `git -C .tickets-tracker diff --name-only --diff-filter=A C0..origin/tickets | grep -E '/[0-9].*\\.json$' | grep -v -- '-SNAPSHOT.json'`
      yields only files whose JSON has a non-empty `author_sig`.
      This confirms bug `0ba4`'s signing fix is LIVE across every writer build (local/CI/Gerrit,
      incl. the enrich drain + reconciler) so the flip breaks nothing. Record `C0`, the window,
      and the per-writer signed events observed. If ANY active process still emits an unsigned
      non-SNAPSHOT event, DO NOT FLIP — remediate that writer first.
- [ ] **The flip is atomic and pins the 8d91 anchor.** `identity.require_authenticated = true`
      **and** `enforce_since = 8dcef776c3dd9e68935b74b82137e1ffd8f04f10` land in **one** change; `enforce_since` is written here
      for the first time (no earlier child modifies it) and its value **equals the exact anchor
      SHA documented by 8d91**. Proving command:
      `python -c "import rebar._config_schema as c, rebar.config as cfg; k=cfg.load_config('.').identity; import sys; sys.exit(0 if (k.require_authenticated and k.enforce_since=='8dcef776c3dd9e68935b74b82137e1ffd8f04f10') else 1)"`
      exits 0 (and `git show HEAD:rebar.toml` contains both on the flip commit).
- [ ] **Enforcement is real, not advisory.** After the flip, `rebar verify-identity
      --all --require-authenticated` returns **non-zero** on a new in-scope unsigned/forged event
      (demonstrated in an isolated temp store via 8d91's harness), and the local write-gate
      **refuses** an unsignable non-exempt write with the signing key unset. Both exits recorded.
- [ ] **Under enforcement, every in-scope writer surface passes.** A representative signed write
      on **local** and **CI** shows `authorship: {signed: >=1}` and verifies (its event uuid is
      absent from `rebar verify-identity --all --format json`); the **Gerrit-side** writer passes
      iff 1a51 classified one (it did — the review-bot LINK). Evidence (ticket ids + event uuids)
      recorded on this ticket. No `main` push goes red.
- [ ] **A documented rollback path exists.** The runbook records the single fast-revert change
      (set `identity.require_authenticated = false`, land through Gerrit) to use if a writer
      surface unexpectedly fails post-flip. [operator-attested] the rollback step is captured in
      the runbook (recorded as a comment on this ticket with the exact revert diff + procedure).

file_impact.reason: the identity.require_authenticated flip + enforce_since anchor pin

compiled_state.title: Flip identity.require_authenticated=true with pinned enforce_since in one change
